title: Merck Q4 and Full-Year 2025 Earnings Highlights
text: Merck reported fourth-quarter 2025 worldwide sales of $16.4 billion, up 5% year over year, and full-year 2025 worldwide sales of $65.0 billion, up 1%. Full-year GAAP EPS was $7.28, and non-GAAP EPS was $8.98. KEYTRUDA and KEYTRUDA QLEX remained Merck's largest growth driver, with full-year sales of $31.7 billion, up 7%. WINREVAIR contributed $1.4 billion of sales, and CAPVAXIVE contributed $759 million, showing increasing contributions from newer launches. Animal Health sales were $6.4 billion, up 8%, supported by livestock demand, supply improvements, and new products. A key weakness was GARDASIL and GARDASIL 9, where full-year sales declined 39% to $5.2 billion, mainly due to lower demand in China and other market pressures. Merck also highlighted pipeline progress, acquisitions of Verona Pharma and Cidara Therapeutics, and 2026 sales guidance of $65.5 billion to $67.0 billion.
